title: Top bar with free-template link
question: Should the explorer have a top bar, and what goes in it?
rationale: Add a top bar with the 'Decision Explorer' wordmark on the left and a 'Get the free template' link to the repo on the right, folding the existing theme and help buttons into it. Because the viewer is one shared template (d28), every published explorer quietly points people to where they can get the tool for free. The icon buttons move into the bar so nothing floats or overlaps.

options.label: Top bar: wordmark + 'Get the free template' link

tradeoffs.note: every shared explorer links back to the free template, so the viewer markets itself
adds a header the calm one-screen app didn't have, but it scrolls away above the sticky toolbar and stays slim
static markup; reuses the existing icon buttons by folding them into the bar

options.label: No top bar (keep floating icon buttons only)

tradeoffs.note: nothing added; the hero stays the first thing seen
no link back to the template; a shared explorer is a dead end for anyone who wants it
